items.title: Iran unveils slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's coffin
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Iran unveiled the coffin of former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in Tehran, as the country's ruling clerics prepare days of mass funeral rites to signal public devotion to the Islamic Republic.

items.title: Dow at record closing high on US jobs data, Nasdaq slips
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>The Dow rose more than ​1% to a record closing high ahead of the long holiday weekend as a softer-than-expected US ‌jobs report eased worries about interest rate hikes, while another sharp drop in chipmaker stocks weighed on the Nasdaq.

items.author: Reuters
items.content:

items.title: Security guard rescued from rubble a week after Venezuela quakes
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Search teams rescued a 44-year-old security guard trapped in the ruins of the mall where he worked in the Venezuelan state of La Guaira, more than a week after two strong earthquakes devastated the country's northern coast.

items.title: Russian daredevils leave court after Empire State Building arrest
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Russian daredevil couple Angela Nikolau and Vanya Beerkus left New York City Criminal Court after their arraignment on July 2. A day earlier, they climbed to the top of the Empire State Building's towering spire to ‌unfurl a large banner urging world peace in what appeared to be an elaborate marriage proposal.

items.author: Reuters
items.content:

items.title: When Russia strikes, Kyiv's volunteers rush into the wreckage
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
